title: Tender Bitter Indian Potato Paratha
description: A tender and flavorful Indian paratha featuring bitter notes, perfect for a brunch mood, made with cardamom and ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly ingredients, the paratha is deglazed to bring out the best flavors.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the whole wheat flour, cardamom powder, and salt.
Gradually add the water to the bowl and knead the dough for about 5 minutes.
Cover the dough with a damp cloth and let it rest for 30 minutes.
Divide the dough into 4 equal parts and roll out each part into a thin circle.
In a separate pan, heat 1 tablespoon of ghee or oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped bitter melon to the pan and cook until it is tender.
Add the boiled and mashed potatoes to the pan and stir well.
Place one of the dough circles in the pan and cook for 1-2 minutes on each side.
Deglaze the pan with a little water to bring out the flavors.
Repeat the process with the remaining dough circles and filling.
Serve the parathas hot with a sprinkle of chopped cilantro.
